primary_method.name: SOTP
primary_method.reasoning: Amazon is a conglomerate with four economically distinct segments: AWS (high-growth cloud infrastructure, 37.7% EBIT margin), Advertising (high-growth platform business, ~55% contribution margin), North America Retail (mature/recovering marketplace, 9% EBIT margin), and International (subscale/building, 3.5% EBIT margin). Blending them into a single DCF or a single multiple is the laziest and most distorting move in valuation — AWS and Advertising compound at 24-28% and carry very different risk and runway than the retail segments. Each is valued on its own method with its own WACC.

segments.name: AWS
segments.reasoning: AWS is a high-growth cloud infrastructure business growing at 28% (Q1 2026, fastest in 15 quarters) with $364bn contracted backlog (+93% YoY). The exit-multiple method is correct: build NOPAT to the normalization year (FY2028, when the AI infrastructure wave matures and growth has decelerated to ~17%), apply the steady-state Gordon multiple, discount back at AWS's WACC. T=2 years (FY2028 from mid-2026 valuation date). NOPAT_T = $230bn FY2028 revenue × 36% EBIT margin × (1 - 21% tax) = $65.4bn. r = 10.5% (CAPM: RFR 4.2%, β 1.15 fundamental — cloud infra business with contracted backlog reducing cyclicality below the broader tech beta, ERP 5.0%: 4.2 + 1.15×5.0 = 9.95% → rounded to 10.5% for conservatism). g_lt = 4.5% (GDP+ for cloud infrastructure in long run; below the ~18% current TAM growth but reflects 10-20yr horizon when cloud penetration matures). ROIC = 50% (asset-light model; AWS generates ~$46bn EBIT on a ~$90bn invested capital base per management disclosures; actual ROIC > 50% but we use 50% as a conservative Gordon input since the formula is not sensitive above 30-40%). The NOPAT path uses the backlog-supported base trajectory: FY2026 $162bn × 36% × 0.79 = $46.0bn; FY2027 $196bn × 36% × 0.79 = $55.7bn; FY2028 $230bn × 36% × 0.79 = $65.4bn.

exit_inputs.reasoning: Normalization year FY2028: AWS revenue $230bn × 36% EBIT margin × (1-21% tax) = $65.4bn NOPAT. Interim FCF is omitted (AWS is a heavy reinvestor; the pure exit-multiple slightly understates by ignoring years 1-2 interim FCF, which is immaterial at this valuation scale and consistent with the heavy capex build cycle in 2026-2027). Gordon: (1 - 0.045/0.50)/(0.10 - 0.045) = 0.91/0.055 = 16.5x exit EV/NOPAT. WACC set at 10.0% = CAPM output (RFR 4.2%, β 1.15 fundamental, ERP 5.0%: 4.2 + 1.15×5.0 = 9.95% rounded to 10.0%). No haircut above CAPM is applied — the contracted backlog already justifies β below the 1.3-1.5 regression beta for AMZN. Exit multiple of 16.5x reconciles to where mature cloud/SaaS peers trade on normalized NOPAT: Azure-equivalent at 15-18x in steady state is industry-consistent. Bias audit: applying a WACC haircut ABOVE CAPM would be a conservative lean without argument; it is removed to satisfy CONTRACT §1.3.

segments.name: Advertising
segments.reasoning: Amazon Advertising is a high-growth digital advertising platform business — ~55% contribution margin, growing 24% YoY to $70bn+ TTM. It behaves like Meta's Family of Apps rather than a retail business: near-zero marginal cost per ad placement, structural share shift from Google as product searches migrate to Amazon. The exit-multiple method is correct; normalization year is FY2027 (when the ramp from Prime Video ads and retail media scaling matures). T=1 year. NOPAT_T = $102bn FY2027 revenue × 55% EBIT margin × (1 - 21% tax) = $44.3bn. r = 9.5% (CAPM: RFR 4.2%, β 1.05 — advertising platform, less cyclical than semis, more cyclical than subscription software: 4.2 + 1.05×5.0 = 9.45%). g_lt = 5.0% (digital advertising grows 12-15% globally; Amazon's share gain is a duration story; by FY2027 the business is at ~$102bn, still only 10% of global digital ad market, implying continued share gain is plausible but the long-run growth rate from that size is nearer nominal GDP+digital share).

exit_inputs.reasoning: FY2026 Advertising revenue $83.8bn × 55% × 0.79 = $36.4bn NOPAT (but using FY2027 as normalization year: $102bn × 55% × 0.79 = $44.3bn). Gordon: (1 - 0.05/0.60)/(0.095 - 0.05) = (1-0.0833)/0.045 = 0.9167/0.045 = 20.4x exit EV/NOPAT — reconciles to Meta Family of Apps at ~18-22x NOPAT on normalized earnings; plausible for a high-margin, high-growth platform with low capital intensity.

segments.name: NA Retail
segments.reasoning: North America retail is a mature/recovering segment: 11% revenue growth, improving margins from automation and advertising mix, but fundamentally a marketplace-and-logistics operation with embedded advertising. Method: FY2027E EBIT × justified multiple. FY2027E EBIT = $519bn revenue × 8.5% margin = $44.1bn. Multiple: 12x EBIT — reflecting the advertising-enhanced margin structure (pure retail without advertising would warrant ~8x; the advertising contribution supports a higher multiple; 12x EBIT is consistent with Walmart at ~15x EBIT for its marketplace/retail operations and slightly discounted for Amazon's retail-concentrated earnings). The 12x is deliberately not the highest defensible number; at 14x the segment value rises ~$56bn.

multiple_inputs.reasoning: FY2027E NA Retail EBIT = $519bn × 8.5% = $44.1bn. Multiple 12x: advertising-enhanced retail margin supports above-Walmart multiple but SOTP context (advertising already valued separately) means the pure retail/logistics multiple is closer to 10-12x. Used 12x as the honest base — not conservative, not heroic.

segments.name: International
segments.reasoning: International is a subscale/building segment: 2.9-3.5% EBIT margin, ongoing investment in India and emerging markets. Method: FY2027E EBIT × discounted multiple. FY2027E EBIT = $210bn revenue × 4.5% normalized margin = $9.5bn. Multiple: 10x EBIT — reflects below-average maturity and ongoing capital intensity; discounted versus NA Retail to recognize the execution risk in building a new geography from subscale positions. At this size and margin the segment is worth between $80-120bn EV, contributing modestly to the SOTP.

multiple_inputs.reasoning: FY2027E International EBIT = $210bn × 4.5% = $9.5bn. Multiple 10x: subscale operation with ongoing investment drag; below NA Retail multiple to reflect execution risk and lower advertising contribution outside the US.

scenarios.label: base
scenarios.reasoning: The argued base: AWS sustains 28% growth in 2026 decelerating to 17% by FY2028 supported by the $364bn contracted backlog; Advertising grows 22% through FY2027; NA Retail expands to 8.5% margin; International reaches 4.5% normalized margin. This is the honest most-likely: it requires only backlog conversion, not AI ROI proof or heroic share gains.

scenarios.label: bull
scenarios.reasoning: AWS sustains 29% growth through mid-2027 before moderating to 22% by FY2028 ($255bn). Advertising accelerates to 24% CAGR ($110bn FY2027). Margins hold as Trainium silicon reduces unit costs. Requires backlog conversion AND new-bookings additions from enterprise AI application layer. The $364bn backlog surge from $244bn is the bull's strongest evidence — extraordinary forward signal.

scenarios.label: bear
scenarios.reasoning: AWS decelerates to 20-22% by late 2026 and 15% by FY2028 ($210bn). Azure's OpenAI advantage pulls enterprise AI budgets away from AWS Bedrock. The $364bn backlog includes renegotiable enterprise commitments; if AI ROI disappoints at scale, enterprise backlog conversion slows. Advertising grows only 18% (Google/Meta response intensifies). Requires Azure to sustain 40%+ share gain momentum for 24+ months.

earnings_bridge.note_anthropic_gain: Q1 2026 EPS of $2.78 includes $16.8bn pre-tax Anthropic mark-to-market gain; clean operating EPS ~$1.55 for Q1 2026. Full-year NTM operating EPS estimate: $7.75 (consensus cluster midpoint post-correction for the ASC 321 artifact).
earnings_bridge.sbc_note: SBC $19.5bn FY2025 / 10.757bn shares = $1.81/share; trending down from $22bn FY2024; charge the cost once — expense SBC, hold shares flat at 10.757bn.
earnings_bridge.owner_earnings_note: Normalized EPS $7.75 - SBC $1.81 = $5.94 owner EPS before capex normalization. During the AI capex build cycle (FY2026 $200bn capex vs $132bn maintenance estimate), incremental capex above maintenance reduces near-term owner FCF to near-zero. Normalized owner FCF post-cycle (FY2028+) estimated at $25-35bn ($2.32-3.25/share) as capex stabilizes near $150bn and CFO grows to $175-200bn.
earnings_bridge.fcf_note: Normalized FCF estimate for FY2028 = CFO ~$185bn - capex ~$155bn stabilized = $30bn / 10.757bn shares = $2.79/share; rounded to $2.90 for FY2028 base. Current FCF is NOT the valuation anchor — the P/FCF of ~332x on FY2025's $7.7bn FCF is meaningless during the capex cycle.

blending_logic: Pure SOTP — sum of segment EVs less parent net debt of $66.2bn. No probability weighting. Base = the argued valuation (#1). Bull/bear are the honest range, not weights.
